[Home](../)

---
title: "HW 7"
format:
  html:
    output-file: "index"
    code-tools: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <script>
          if (window.define) { delete window.define.amd; }
        </script>
embed-resources: true
---

## Exercise 1
```{python}
#| code-fold: true

import altair as alt
import pandas as pd

url = "https://calvin-data304.netlify.app/data/swd-lets-practice-ex-2-3.csv"
df = pd.read_csv(url)
df["capacity"] = df["capacity"].str.replace(",", "").astype(int)
df["demand"] = df["demand"].str.replace(",", "").astype(int)
df["date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"])

long = df.melt(id_vars="date", value_vars=["capacity", "demand"],
               var_name="type", value_name="value")

df2 = df.copy()
df2["unmet"] = df2["demand"] - df2["capacity"]
```

### 1. Grouped Bar
```{python}
#| code-fold: true

chart1 = alt.Chart(long).mark_bar().encode(
    x=alt.X("yearmonth(date):T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("value:Q", title="Value"),
    color="type:N",
    xOffset="type:N"
).properties(width=400, height=200, title="Demand vs Capacity Grouped Bar")

chart1
```

### 2. Line Chart
```{python}
#| code-fold: true

chart2 = alt.Chart(long).mark_line(point=False).encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("value:Q", title="Value"),
    color=alt.Color("type:N", title="Type")
).properties(width=400, height=200, title="Demand vs Capacity Line")

chart2
```

### 3. Overlapping Bars
```{python}
#| code-fold: true

demand_bars = alt.Chart(df).mark_bar(opacity=0.4, color="blue", width=20).encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("demand:Q", title="Value")
)

cap_bars = alt.Chart(df).mark_bar(color="lightblue", width=12).encode(
    x="date:T",
    y="capacity:Q"
)

chart3 = (demand_bars + cap_bars).properties(width=400, height=200, title="Demand vs Capacity Overlapped")

chart3
```

### 4. Stacked Bar
```{python}
#| code-fold: true

stacked = df2.melt(id_vars="date", value_vars=["capacity", "unmet"],
                   var_name="type", value_name="value")

chart4 = alt.Chart(stacked).mark_bar().encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("value:Q", title="Value", stack=True),
    color=alt.Color("type:N", title="Type")
).properties(width=400, height=200, title="Capacity + Unmet Demand")

chart4
```

### 5. Connected Dot
```{python}
#| code-fold: true

rules = alt.Chart(df).mark_rule().encode(
    x="date:T",
    y="capacity:Q",
    y2="demand:Q"
)

cap_pts = alt.Chart(df).mark_point(filled=True, size=60, color="steelblue").encode(
    x="date:T",
    y="capacity:Q"
)

dem_pts = alt.Chart(df).mark_point(filled=True, size=60, color="orange").encode(
    x="date:T",
    y="demand:Q"
)

chart5 = (rules + cap_pts + dem_pts).properties(width=400, height=200, title="Demand vs Capacity")

chart5
```

### 6. Demand Line
```{python}
#| code-fold: true

dem_line = alt.Chart(df2).mark_line(color="steelblue", point=True).encode(
    x="date:T",
    y="demand:Q"
)

chart6 = (dem_line).properties(width=400, height=200, title="Unmet Demand")

chart6
```


## a.
```{python}
#| code-fold: true
jobs_url = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/vega-datasets@2.8.0/data/jobs.json"
jobs = pd.read_json(jobs_url)

alt.Chart(jobs_url).mark_line(point=True).encode(
    x="year:O",
    y="sum(perc):Q",
    color="sex:N"
).properties(width=400, height=200, title="Sum of perc by Year and Sex")
```

The sum of perc for men and women is close to 1.0 each year. This would mean that perc is the proportion of the total population working in the job.

## b. Scatterplot with 4 year facets
```{python}
#| code-fold: true

alt.Chart(jobs_url).transform_filter(
    "datum.year == 1850 || datum.year == 1950 || datum.year == 1980 || datum.year == 2000"
).transform_pivot(
    "sex", groupby=["job", "year"], value="perc"
).mark_point().encode(
    x=alt.X("men:Q", title="Men", axis=alt.Axis(format=".0%")),
    y=alt.Y("women:Q", title="Women", axis=alt.Axis(format=".0%")),
    tooltip=["job:N", "men:Q", "women:Q"],
    column="year:O"
).properties(width=200, height=200)
```

### c. Percentage within each sex
```{python}
#| code-fold: true

filtered = jobs[jobs["year"].isin([1850, 1950, 1980, 2000])].copy()
totals = filtered.groupby(["year", "sex"])["count"].transform("sum")
filtered["pct"] = filtered["count"] / totals

alt.Chart(filtered).transform_pivot(
    "sex", groupby=["job", "year"], value="pct"
).mark_point().encode(
    x=alt.X("men:Q", title="% of Men", axis=alt.Axis(format=".0%")),
    y=alt.Y("women:Q", title="% of Women", axis=alt.Axis(format=".0%")),
    tooltip=["job:N", "men:Q", "women:Q"],
    column="year:O"
).properties(width=200, height=200)
```
